seo.title: Llama Stack Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Llama Stack agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams deploying Llama Stack agents for resume screening and candidate evaluation face heightened compliance risks due to strict anti-discrimination laws like EEOC guidelines and the EU AI Act. False positives from safety shields can unfairly filter diverse candidates, while hallucinated memory retrievals may associate protected characteristics with hiring decisions, triggering lawsuits and fines up to $1,500 per violation. Vex provides essential guardrails to ensure Llama Stack outputs align with regulatory standards for fair, auditable AI hiring.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits AI tools from screening based on protected characteristics like race, gender, age, or disability; requires evidence of non-discriminatory impact.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies agent responses against bias patterns and protected class mentions, flagging disparate treatment before candidate decisions are made.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates bias audits for automated employment decision tools and public disclosure of AI usage in hiring.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex logs all verifications with audit trails, enabling annual compliance reports and proof of bias mitigation.

complianceRequirements.description: Classifies hiring AI as high-risk, requiring risk assessments, transparency, and human oversight.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces schema validation on tool outputs and resolution checks, ensuring transparent, auditable agent behavior.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires reasonable accommodations and prohibits discrimination based on disability or other protected traits.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex detects and blocks responses referencing disabilities or accommodations in screening, preventing inadvertent bias.

industryRisks.name: Safety Shield False Positives
industryRisks.description: Llama Stack's safety filters incorrectly flag benign resume content (e.g., 'women's health experience') as violations, excluding qualified diverse candidates.
industryRisks.example: Female nurse practitioner resume rejected due to 'pregnancy' mention in clinical history.

industryRisks.name: Agentic Loop Turn Limit Exceeded
industryRisks.description: Unresolved candidate evaluation loops exceed limits without actionable hiring recommendations.
industryRisks.example: Resume screening agent loops indefinitely on ambiguous skills, delaying 500+ applications.

industryRisks.name: Tool Schema Non-Conformance
industryRisks.description: Recruitment tools return unstructured data, leading to inconsistent candidate scoring.
industryRisks.example: Candidate ranker tool outputs free-text instead of JSON scores, breaking downstream HR systems.

industryRisks.name: Memory Hallucination
industryRisks.description: Agent fabricates document associations, linking candidates to irrelevant or biased prior records.
industryRisks.example: Associates applicant with 'former felon' memory from unrelated resume, auto-rejecting them.

implementation.title: Wrap Llama Stack Responses with Vex.verify()
implementation.description: Post-process every agent turn to catch bias, schema errors, and hallucinations before HR decisions.

implementation.title: Audit Memory Retrievals for Hallucinations
implementation.description: Validate Llama Stack memory bank outputs against source documents to prevent fabricated associations.

implementation.title: Enforce Loop Resolution & Notification
implementation.description: Ensure agent loops terminate with decisions and include mandatory AI disclosure notices.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Llama Stack in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: TechCo deployed a Llama Stack agent to screen 10,000 engineering resumes monthly. The agent used safety shields, memory banks of past hires, and ranking tools to score candidates. Within weeks, HR noticed disproportionate rejection of older candidates (over 40) and women from certain universities, despite qualifications. Safety filters flagged 'veteran' mentions as violence risks, memory retrieval hallucinated prior rejections incorrectly, and tool outputs lacked consistent scoring schemas. An EEOC complaint emerged after 15 rejected candidates sued for age and gender discrimination.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Integrating Vex.verify() caught 87% of biased outputs pre-deployment. Safety shield false positives dropped 95% via custom HR rules, memory hallucinations were blocked with document verification, and schema enforcement ensured auditable scores. TechCo passed their annual bias audit, notified candidates of AI usage automatically, and diversified their engineering hires by 23%. No lawsuits materialized, saving millions in potential fines and legal fees.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and wrap your Llama Stack agents to eliminate bias risks and ensure regulatory compliance.
